tempPoint.description: A published development time at one temperature. Times are only ever shown for temperatures a manufacturer datasheet supports (interpolated within published points, never extrapolated).

tempC10.description: Temperature in tenths of a degree Celsius (20.0 C = 200).

timeSec.description: Development time in seconds at this temperature.

agitation.description: Agitation scheme, structured so a timer can drive a metronome.

initialSec.description: Continuous agitation at the start of the step.

everySec.description: Agitation cycle period (e.g. 60 = once a minute).

main.description: A development recipe: the time(s) to develop a given film at a given exposure index in a given developer/dilution, sourced from a manufacturer datasheet. Bundled curated recipes and user-saved personal recipes share this shape.

ei.description: Exposure index the time is for; push/pull rows are separate recipes.

temps.description: One or more published (temperature, time) points. Multiple points enable interpolation between them.

source.description: URL of the manufacturer datasheet this recipe was transcribed from.
